canonical: Block git commits until the required repository validation commands for the changed files have run after the last relevant write.

example_repos.quote: - Run `gofmt`/`goimports` before committing backend changes
- Always run tests before committing. The test command is `uv run prek run --all-files`.
**Tests must pass and lint/typing must be clean before committing.**
Always run lint and typecheck before committing code to ensure quality.

dsl:
source gate validation := exec(argv matches "^(test|pytest|go test|npm|pnpm|bun|make|just|pre-commit|ruff|mypy|tsc|tsgo|gofmt|goimports|black|spotless)")
rule R01 deny exec "git" @arg "commit" if target-scope(changed-files) unless after exec validation since last-write(target-scope(changed-files))
reason "commits require fresh repository validation"
effect kill

canonical: Block commits or pushes when a repository requires pre-commit or equivalent hooks and those hooks have not been installed or run successfully.

example_repos.quote: IMPORTANT: Before making any changes to the codebase, ALWAYS run `make install-pre-commit-hooks` to ensure pre-commit hooks are properly installed.
The pre-commit hooks MUST pass successfully before pushing any changes to the repository. This is a mandatory requirement to maintain code quality and consistency.
- **Run pre-commit hooks** before making PRs
- **Pre-commit hooks:** ruff + isort — run `pre-commit install` before committing

dsl:
source gate precommit_ok := exec(argv matches "(pre-commit|prek|lefthook|install-pre-commit-hooks)")
rule R02 deny exec "git" @arg "commit|push" unless after exec precommit_ok since last-write(target-scope(changed-files))
reason "repository hook gate must run before VCS mutation"
effect kill

canonical: Block committing or pushing files labeled as secrets, credentials, API keys, cookies, real personal data, or .env material.

example_repos.quote: - Never commit secrets or credentials
- NEVER commit secrets, credentials, or .env files
- Never commit secrets - use environment variables or secure vaults
- Never commit secrets, personal data, or real identity information (see `@docs/book/src/contributing/privacy.md`).

dsl:
source file "**/.env*" label secret
source file "**/*" label secret if name-or-content matches "(?i)(api[_-]?key|token|secret|credential|cookie|private[_-]?key|personal data|real phone|real identity)"
rule R03 deny exec "git" @arg "commit|push" if staged-files has_label secret
reason "secret-labeled material must not enter version control"
effect kill

canonical: Block git pushes and PR-shipping commands until required tests, lint, typecheck, build, or proof gates have run after the last relevant write.

example_repos.quote: - Validate changes with `go test ./... -race -cover` before pushing
The pre-commit hooks MUST pass successfully before pushing any changes to the repository. This is a mandatory requirement to maintain code quality and consistency.
**Always run these scripts before pushing code or creating a PR:**
- Run `make format`, `make lint`, `make typecheck`, and `make tests` before marking work ready.

dsl:
source gate push_validation := exec(argv matches "(test|lint|typecheck|build|verify|pre-commit|mypy|pytest|go test|make|just|bun|pnpm|npm)")
rule R04 deny exec "git" @arg "push" if target-scope(changed-files) unless after exec push_validation since last-write(target-scope(changed-files))
reason "pushes require fresh validation evidence"
effect kill

canonical: Block repo-forbidden full, root, local, or expensive validation sweeps unless an end-of-task, scope-required, CI-reproduction, or explicit approval gate endorses the run.

example_repos.quote: Never run root `bun test`; the root script prints `do not run tests from root` and exits with code 1. Use package-level tests instead.
**Never run tests locally.** All tests (E2E, UI, python socket tests) run via GitHub Actions or on the VM.

- Never run the full vitest suite directly: it includes e2e tests that activate when endpoint/auth env vars are present. For all non-e2e tests, run `./test.sh` from the repo root. Otherwise run specific tests from the package root: `node ../../node_modules/vitest/dist/cli.js --run test/specific.test
- **Build**: Inspect `Makefile` and `.github/workflows/` — ask the user before running long builds

dsl:
endorse expensive-validation-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "full-test|long-build|optional-sweep|end-of-task"
rule R05 deny exec "bun|npm|pnpm|make|pytest|vitest" @arg "test|typecheck|bazel_lint_changed|build" if target-scope(repo-root OR full-suite) unless endorsed expensive-validation-approved
reason "forbidden or expensive validation sweeps require the proper gate"
effect kill

canonical: Block git add, commit, push, or PR creation unless the user-request or active-command approval gate has endorsed that VCS action.

example_repos.quote: - Never commit unless explicitly requested.
- Never commit unless the user asks.
- **YOU MUST NOT commit or push without explicit user request.**
- **Git:** Never git add, commit, push, or create PR unless the user explicitly asks, or the active command/skill explicitly requires it.

dsl:
endorse vcs-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "git|commit|push|pr"
rule R06 deny exec "git|gh" @arg "add|commit|push|pr create" unless endorsed vcs-approved
reason "VCS mutation requires explicit user or active-command approval"
effect kill

canonical: Block git commits whose message argv does not satisfy the repository-required conventional or explicit commit-message format.

example_repos.quote: Conventional Commits required. Enforced by commitlint via prek `commit-msg` hook.
PRs are squash-merged, so the PR title becomes the commit message that release-please parses. Always use [Conventional Commit](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) prefixes in PR titles.
- Commit messages and PR titles must follow Conventional Commits:
Commit format: `<type>(<scope>): <subject>` in English. Types: feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, test, style, perf. **NEVER add AI signatures** (Co-Authored-By, Generated with, etc.).

dsl:
source repo label conventional_commit_required
rule R07 deny exec "git" @arg "commit" if repo has_label conventional_commit_required unless argv.message matches "^(feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|test|chore|perf)(\(.+\))?: .+"
reason "commit message must follow the required conventional format"
effect kill

canonical: Block writing secret-tainted data to durable files or process output unless it has passed a redaction or declassification gate.

example_repos.quote: CRITICAL: Never write raw sensitive data to durable artifacts. This includes passwords, API keys, bearer tokens, SNMP communities, private keys, connection strings with embedded credentials, session cookies, community member names, customer names, customer identifiers, personal data, non-private IP
- Live-verify when feasible. Never print secrets.
- NEVER hardcode API keys in scripts or source files
cookies, project keys. Never commit secrets; never hard-code tokens in scripts.

dsl:
source file "**/.env*" label secret
source env "*_KEY|*_TOKEN|*_SECRET|*_PASSWORD|*_COOKIE" label secret
declassify secret on exec "redact-secrets|scrub-secrets|actplane-declassify" @arg "secret"
rule R08 deny write file "**" if writer tainted secret unless declassified secret
reason "raw secret-tainted bytes cannot be written to durable artifacts or logs"
effect kill

canonical: Block committing or pushing directly on protected main or master branches; work must go through a non-protected branch and PR flow.

example_repos.quote: - Don't commit to `main` directly - branch off `beta` for PRs
- Always new branch for changes, PR to main, never push to main directly
- Do not commit directly to the `main` branch. All changes should be made in a separate branch and go through a pull request.
- Work from a non-`master` branch. Open a PR to `master`; do not push directly.

dsl:
source branch protected := "main|master"
rule R09 deny exec "git" @arg "commit|push" if current-branch matches protected
reason "protected branches require branch-and-PR workflow"
effect kill

canonical: Block committing temporary, generated, release-note, provider-shim, PR-asset, or CI-controlled output paths unless a generator gate created and approved them.

example_repos.quote: - Do not commit the release note markdown file. Keep it untracked and use it directly for the tag annotation and GitHub Release body.
- Do not commit generated provider config or shim outputs under `.claude/`,
- PR artifacts/screenshots: attach to PR/comment/external artifact store. Do not commit `.github/pr-assets`.

- Templates: `templates/**` is CI-controlled output. Never manually edit or commit template source, manifests, or lockfiles. Fix the source registry, package, or workflow inputs and let CI regenerate templates. If local verification rewrites template files, restore them before handoff.

dsl:
source file "release-notes/**|.claude/**|.github/pr-assets/**|.pr/**|templates/**" label generated_artifact
source gate generator_ok := exec(argv matches "(build|generate|brl|provider-config|release-note)")
rule R10 deny exec "git" @arg "add|commit" if target-scope(staged-files) has_label generated_artifact unless after exec generator_ok since last-write(target-scope(staged-files))
reason "generated and temporary artifacts require their generator gate before commit"
effect kill

canonical: Block dependency additions, version bumps, overrides, vendored dependency changes, build-script allowlists, or major selections unless dependency approval is endorsed.

example_repos.quote: - No new dependencies without an explicit request.
- Do not update dependencies casually. Version bumps, patched dependencies, overrides, or vendored dependency changes require explicit approval.
- Dependency patches/overrides/vendor changes need explicit approval. `pnpm-workspace.yaml` patched dependencies use exact versions only.
- **`onlyBuiltDependencies`**: Never add packages to this list without human approval — build scripts execute arbitrary code during install.

dsl:
source file "package.json|pnpm-lock.yaml|package-lock.json|yarn.lock|bun.lock|uv.lock|requirements*.txt|pyproject.toml|vendor/**|pnpm-workspace.yaml" label dependency_surface
endorse deps-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "dependencies"
rule R11 deny write file dependency_surface unless endorsed deps-approved
reason "dependency and vendored-surface changes require approval"
effect kill

canonical: Block external network connections during ordinary tests unless the process is running under an integration-test gate that permits external services.

example_repos.quote: These should not assume any Onyx/external services are available to be called.
- **Only mock at system boundaries** - external APIs, databases, file systems, network calls, and other I/O with side effects.
Integration tests verify Vector works with real external services. Require Docker or Podman.
- For SDK integration tests requiring external services, document any required keys in the PR.

dsl:
source process label ordinary_test if lineage-includes exec "test|pytest|vitest|go test|bun test|npm test"
endorse integration_network on exec "actplane-approve|docker|podman" @arg "integration-test|external-service"
rule R12 deny connect ipv4 "0.0.0.0/0" if process has_label ordinary_test unless endorsed integration_network
reason "ordinary tests must not call external services"
effect kill

canonical: Block network egress from processes tainted by raw secrets unless an approved secret-handling gate declassifies the flow.

example_repos.quote: - When explaining environment interpolation, use placeholders and fenced code blocks. Never include raw `.env` contents in outbound comments.
- **Credential Management**: Never log, commit, or expose API keys, tokens, or sensitive configuration
(LLM config, secrets) securely via `LookupSecret`. Raw secret values only flow SaaS→sandbox,

Public-skill scripts that touch credentials (cloud tokens, per-agent bearers, claim ids, session cookies) MUST be **token-safe** -- helpers that handle credential bytes are named with a leading underscore (`_skill_*`, internal-only) and return them via bash namerefs into the caller's local variables

dsl:
source file "**/.env*" label secret
source env "*_KEY|*_TOKEN|*_SECRET|*_PASSWORD|*_COOKIE" label secret
declassify secret on exec "lookup-secret|token-safe-wrapper|actplane-declassify"
rule R13 deny connect ipv4 "0.0.0.0/0" if process tainted secret unless declassified secret
reason "secret-tainted processes cannot make outbound network connections without an approved secret path"
effect kill

canonical: Block writing secret-tainted API keys or credentials into source or script files; they must stay in environment variables, env files, or approved vault paths.

example_repos.quote: - API keys only from environment variables, never hardcoded
- Always source credentials from environment at runtime
Never commit API keys. Use env vars: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
- Never commit secrets - use environment variables or secure vaults

dsl:
source file "**/.env*" label secret
source env "*_KEY|*_TOKEN|*_SECRET|*_PASSWORD" label secret
rule R14 deny write file "**/*.{js,ts,py,go,rs,sh,yml,yaml,json}" if writer tainted secret unless target-scope("**/.env*" OR "**/*vault*" OR "**/*secret*example*")
reason "credential bytes must not be hardcoded into source or scripts"
effect kill

canonical: Block staging or committing files outside the current task scope or user-approved change set.

example_repos.quote: - When the user says `commit`, commit only your changes. When the user says `commit all`, commit everything in grouped chunks.

Files in git commit should only be related to the current modification task. Environment modifications for running (e.g., `conf/`, `AGENTS.md`, `hooks/`, etc.) must not be `git add`ed. When delivering the final task, you must ensure all actual code modifications have been committed.
- Before committing, run `git status` and verify you are only staging your files.
- Commit via `scripts/committer "<msg>" <file...>`; stage intended files only.

dsl:
source label task_scope from target-scope(current-task)
endorse all-changes-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "commit all"
rule R15 deny exec "git" @arg "add|commit" if target-scope(staged-files) NOT task_scope unless endorsed all-changes-approved
reason "commits may include only task-scoped or explicitly approved files"
effect kill

canonical: Block commit messages that add Co-Authored-By, Generated with, or other AI-signature trailers.

example_repos.quote: - Do not add Co-Authored-By to any commit.
Commit format: `<type>(<scope>): <subject>` in English. Types: feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, test, style, perf. **NEVER add AI signatures** (Co-Authored-By, Generated with, etc.).
- **YOU MUST NOT add `Co-Authored-By: Claude`** to any commit message.
Never add Co-Authored-By agents in commit message.

dsl:
rule R16 deny exec "git" @arg "commit" if argv contains "Co-Authored-By|Generated with|Claude|AI"
reason "AI attribution trailers are forbidden in commit messages"
effect kill

canonical: Block writes or unlinks to paths labeled read-only reference, installed read-only tools, plugin core, or deferred-write skill areas.

example_repos.quote: - Vendor submodules in `ts/vendor/` are **read-only reference only** — do not modify them
- `ts/vendor/effect/` and `ts/vendor/clack/` are **read-only reference submodules** — npm provides the actual deps.
**Rule (Teknium, May 2026):** plugins MUST NOT modify core files

Status: live. SKILL.md plus `scripts/_lib.sh` (token-safe wrappers + sentinel no-leak self-test), `scripts/analyze-local.sh`, `scripts/pr-issues.sh`, and a live `how-tos/INDEX.md` catalog. Read-only by design; write actions deferred to a future SOW.

dsl:
source file "ts/vendor/**|**/vendor/**|core/**|registry-installed/**|skills/**" label readonly_reference
rule R17 deny write file readonly_reference
rule R17 deny unlink file readonly_reference
reason "read-only reference and installed tool paths cannot be mutated"
effect kill

canonical: Block lockfile writes or commits unless the matching package-manager or repository lockfile gate generated them.

example_repos.quote: When regenerating lockfiles (poetry.lock, uv.lock, etc.), you MUST use the same tool version that originally generated the lockfile to avoid unnecessary diff noise. Each lockfile contains a version header indicating which tool version was used.
Always commit the refreshed `uv.lock` when dependencies shift, and mention optional groups (VERL, APO, GPU) in PR notes.

- **Adding or bumping a runtime dep in `container/agent-runner/`** → edit `package.json`, then `cd container/agent-runner && bun install` and commit the updated `bun.lock`. Do not run `pnpm install` there — agent-runner is not a pnpm workspace.
- Pre-commit blocks lockfile commits unless `PI_ALLOW_LOCKFILE_CHANGE=1`. Don't bypass unless the user wants the lockfile change committed.

dsl:
source file "**/{poetry.lock,uv.lock,bun.lock,pnpm-lock.yaml,package-lock.json,yarn.lock}" label lockfile
source gate lock_tool_ok := exec(argv matches "(poetry|uv|bun install|pnpm install|npm install|yarn install|pre-commit)")
rule R18 deny write file lockfile unless after exec lock_tool_ok since last-write(target-scope(lockfile))
rule R18 deny exec "git" @arg "add|commit" if target-scope(staged-files) has_label lockfile unless after exec lock_tool_ok
reason "lockfiles must be generated by the correct tool gate"
effect kill

canonical: Block destructive file or data deletion operations unless the target is explicitly labeled archive-safe or a destructive confirmation gate endorses the action.

example_repos.quote: - Never deletes; max destructive action is archive.

- **Never delete R2 data**: The mount directory `/data/moltbot` IS the R2 bucket. Running `rm -rf /data/moltbot/*` will DELETE your backup data. Always check mount status before any destructive operations.

**LLM data is never deleted.** All LLM output — context fed to the model, reasoning, tool calls, messages, events, steps — is the most valuable data in the system. Never strip, truncate, or delete it from the database. Mark with timestamps, make filterable, but always retain. In-memory HashMaps are
3. After completing tests, do not drop tables; instead drop tables before using them in tests, to preserve the environment for debugging

dsl:
source file "/data/moltbot/**|**/llm/**|**/messages/**|**/events/**|**/db/**" label protected_data
endorse destructive-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "destructive"
rule R19 deny unlink file protected_data unless endorsed destructive-approved
rule R19 deny exec "rm|rmdir|psql|mysql|sqlite3" @arg "-rf|DROP|TRUNCATE|DELETE" if target-scope(protected_data) unless endorsed destructive-approved
reason "protected data cannot be deleted without destructive confirmation"
effect kill

canonical: Block destructive git history operations such as reset --hard or history rewrites unless an explicit destructive-git approval gate endorsed them.

example_repos.quote: | **Off-limits** | Real `.env` / secrets, production credentials, unrelated repos, destructive git ops without confirmation. |
- Do not use destructive git commands such as `reset --hard` unless explicitly

If sensitive data is required to continue, stop and ask the user for a secure handling path. If sensitive data is found in a durable artifact, sanitize it before any commit. If sensitive data was already committed, tell the user and do not rewrite history without explicit approval.
- Do not use destructive git commands such as `reset --hard` unless explicitly

dsl:
endorse destructive-git-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "destructive-git"
rule R20 deny exec "git" @arg "reset --hard|rebase|filter-branch|push --force|push -f" unless endorsed destructive-git-approved
reason "destructive git history changes require explicit approval"
effect kill

canonical: Block committing GitHub Actions workflow edits unless an action-pin verification gate has run for full-length SHA-pinned third-party actions.

example_repos.quote: - Pin external third-party actions to a full 40-character commit SHA, with the version tag in a trailing comment (e.g. `uses: owner/repo@<sha> # v1.2.3`). Do not use mutable tags (`@v1`) or branches for third-party actions.

This repository require actions to be pinned to a full-length commit SHA. Attempting to use a tag will fail. Use the `gh` cli to query. Verify tags are not annotated tag objects (which would need dereferencing).

This repository require actions to be pinned to a full-length commit SHA. Attempting to use a tag will fail. Use the `gh` cli to query. Verify tags are not annotated tag objects (which would need dereferencing).
All GitHub Actions in `.github/workflows/` are pinned to commit SHAs. Before shipping

dsl:
source file ".github/workflows/**" label workflow
source gate actions_pin_ok := exec(argv matches "(actionlint|pin-actions|verify-actions-sha)")
rule R21 deny exec "git" @arg "add|commit|push" if target-scope(staged-files) has_label workflow unless after exec actions_pin_ok since last-write(target-scope(staged-files))
reason "workflow changes require full-SHA action pin verification"
effect kill

canonical: Block direct or handwritten test execution paths when the repository requires preset test scripts, harnesses, recording commands, or verification profiles.

example_repos.quote: You must use the preset scripts in the codebase with their correct parameters to run tests (`run-regression-test.sh`, `run-be-ut.sh`, `run-fe-ut.sh`). Regression test result files must not be handwritten; they must be auto-generated via test scripts. When running regression tests, if using `-s` to s
- `pnpm harness` is a command that gets the API server and workers up for you to run the tests. Don't try to `pnpm start` manually.
| Recording integration tests | MUST use the recording command in `docs/agents/testing-flow.md` -> `Integration tests` (not `-record`; `-record` is for unit-test suites that explicitly support it). |

| Claiming task completion / PR readiness | MUST use the `Ready` verification profile from `.agents/skills/tidb-verify-profile`; if there are code changes, this includes `make lint`. `Ready` is mandatory before making final-status claims such as "fixed", "done", "all tests pass", "ready for review",

dsl:
source gate prescribed_test := exec(argv matches "(run-regression-test.sh|run-be-ut.sh|run-fe-ut.sh|pnpm harness|Ready|tidb-verify-profile|recording command)")
rule R22 deny exec "pytest|go|pnpm|npm|bun|make" @arg "test|start|-record" if target-scope(test) unless lineage-includes exec prescribed_test
rule R22 deny write file "**/test-results/**|**/regression-results/**" unless lineage-includes exec prescribed_test
reason "tests must use repository-prescribed harnesses and result generators"
effect kill

canonical: Block force-push operations on git remotes.

example_repos.quote: - **NEVER** force-push on collaborative repos
- Never force push.
- **NEVER** force-push on collaborative repos

dsl:
rule R23 deny exec "git" @arg "push" if argv contains "--force|--force-with-lease|-f"
reason "force-push is forbidden"
effect kill

canonical: Block git commits in signoff-required repositories unless the commit argv includes -s or --signoff.

example_repos.quote: - **Sign commits**: Use `git commit --signoff` as required by contributor guidelines.
- DCO sign-off: All commits MUST use the `-s` flag (otherwise CI will reject them)
- **Sign commits**: Use `git commit --signoff` as required by contributor guidelines.
- DCO sign-off: All commits MUST use the `-s` flag (otherwise CI will reject them)

dsl:
source repo label signoff_required
rule R24 deny exec "git" @arg "commit" if repo has_label signoff_required unless argv contains "-s|--signoff"
reason "DCO/signoff commits must use -s or --signoff"
effect kill

canonical: Block manual release, publish, tag, and hand-rolled ship command sequences unless they run through the approved release gate.

example_repos.quote: **Do not run `npm publish`, `git push`, or `git tag` yourself** — these are
**NEVER hand-roll ship operations.** Do not manually run git commit + push + gh pr

dsl:
endorse release-approved on exec "release-please|ship|actplane-approve" @arg "release"
rule R25 deny exec "npm|pnpm|bun|git|gh" @arg "publish|tag|commit|push|pr create" if target-scope(release) unless lineage-includes exec release-approved
reason "release operations must use the approved release path"
effect kill

canonical: Block CI/CD workflow changes unless a CI-workflow approval gate has endorsed them.

example_repos.quote: - Modify CI/CD workflows without explicit approval.
- Modify CI/CD workflows without explicit approval.

- For new feature requirements or requirement-related tasks involving `node:*` built-in modules, DOM operations, Vue composables, React hooks, Vite plugins, or GitHub Actions workflows, always do deep research for suitable existing libraries or open source modules first. Before choosing any library,

dsl:
source file ".github/workflows/**|.gitlab-ci.yml|circle.yml|azure-pipelines.yml" label ci_workflow
endorse ci-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "ci-workflow"
rule R26 deny write file ci_workflow unless endorsed ci-approved
reason "CI/CD workflow edits require explicit approval"
effect kill

canonical: Block file writes, unlinks, and mutating VCS commands from agents running in read-only, reviewer, or reconnaissance modes.

example_repos.quote: - **Modes**: Three modes — Plan (read-only investigation), Agent (tool use with approval), YOLO (auto-approved). See `docs/MODES.md` for details.
A separate **reviewer** graph runs read-only code reviews on PRs, and a **review-style analyzer** graph learns per-repo review style from historical PRs.
| `reviewer` | `agent.reviewer:get_reviewer_agent` | Read-only PR reviewer. Findings model + `publish_review`. |
| `reviewer` | `agent.reviewer:get_reviewer_agent` | Read-only PR reviewer. Findings model + `publish_review`. |

dsl:
source process label readonly_mode if lineage-includes exec "reviewer|plan|agent_open --readonly"
rule R27 deny write file "**" if process has_label readonly_mode
rule R27 deny unlink file "**" if process has_label readonly_mode
rule R27 deny exec "git" @arg "add|commit|push" if process has_label readonly_mode
reason "read-only agent modes cannot mutate files or VCS state"
effect kill

canonical: Block manual edits to generated migration and database snapshot paths unless the repository migration generator ran after the schema change.

example_repos.quote: - **NEVER manually edit files in `packages/db/drizzle/`** - this includes `.sql` migration files, `meta/_journal.json`, and snapshot files. These are auto-generated by Drizzle. If you need to create a migration, only modify the schema files in `packages/db/src/schema/` and ask the user to run `drizz

- LangBot uses [Alembic](https://alembic.sqlalchemy.org/) to manage database migrations, supporting both SQLite and PostgreSQL. Migration files are located in `src/langbot/pkg/persistence/alembic/versions/`. If you changed the definition of database entities (ORM models), generate a new migration sc

- LangBot uses [Alembic](https://alembic.sqlalchemy.org/) to manage database migrations, supporting both SQLite and PostgreSQL. Migration files are located in `src/langbot/pkg/persistence/alembic/versions/`. If you changed the definition of database entities (ORM models), generate a new migration sc

dsl:
source file "packages/db/drizzle/**|**/alembic/versions/**|**/migrations/**" label generated_migration
source gate migration_generator := exec(argv matches "(drizzle|alembic revision|makemigrations|migration generate)")
rule R28 deny write file generated_migration unless after exec migration_generator since last-write(target-scope(schema-files))
reason "migration artifacts must be produced through the generator"
effect kill

canonical: Block secret-tainted or interpolation-sensitive comment bodies from being passed inline through shell argv to outbound collaboration tools.

example_repos.quote: - Never pass GitHub, Linear, or Slack comment bodies inline through shell arguments when the body contains `$`, `${...}`, backticks, `$(...)`, environment-variable examples, secrets, or config interpolation examples.
- When explaining environment interpolation, use placeholders and fenced code blocks. Never include raw `.env` contents in outbound comments.

- Never pass GitHub, Linear, or Slack comment bodies inline through shell arguments when the body contains `$`, `${...}`, backticks, `$(...)`, environment-variable examples, secrets, or config interpolation examples.
- When explaining environment interpolation, use placeholders and fenced code blocks. Never include raw `.env` contents in outbound comments.

dsl:
source file "**/.env*" label secret
source file "**" label shell_interpolation if content matches "[$`]|\$\(|\$\{"
rule R29 deny exec "gh|slack|linear" @arg tainted(secret OR shell_interpolation)
reason "sensitive or shell-active comment bodies must not travel inline in argv"
effect kill

canonical: Block edits to baseline, inventory, ignore, snapshot, or expected-failure files used to silence checks unless a baseline approval gate endorses the edit.

example_repos.quote: - Do not edit baseline/inventory/ignore/snapshot/expected-failure files to silence checks without explicit approval.
- Do not edit baseline/inventory/ignore/snapshot/expected-failure files to silence checks without explicit approval.

dsl:
source file "**/{baseline,inventory,ignore,snapshot,expected-failure,expected_failures}**" label check_silencing
endorse baseline-approved on exec "actplane-approve" @arg "baseline"
rule R30 deny write file check_silencing unless endorsed baseline-approved
reason "check-silencing files require explicit approval"
effect kill

canonical: Block harness agents from writing outside their designated workspace root.

example_repos.quote: An agent operating the harness only edits inside `agent-workspace/`:

dsl:
source file "agent-workspace/**" label workspace_allowed
rule R31 deny write file "**" if process has_label harness_agent AND target-scope(path) NOT workspace_allowed
rule R31 deny unlink file "**" if process has_label harness_agent AND target-scope(path) NOT workspace_allowed
reason "harness agents may edit only inside the assigned workspace"
effect kill

canonical: Block test processes from writing to user home configuration directories that the repository marks as non-test output.

example_repos.quote: ### Tests must not write to `~/.hermes/`

dsl:
source process label test_run if lineage-includes exec "test|pytest|vitest|bun test|go test"
source file "~/.hermes/**|~/.config/**|~/.ssh/**" label home_config
rule R32 deny write file home_config if process has_label test_run
reason "tests must not mutate user home configuration"
effect kill

canonical: Block raw GitHub PR or issue creation unless the designated repository skill is in the exec lineage.

example_repos.quote: When creating a Pull Request, you MUST use the `gh-create-pr` skill.
When creating an Issue, you MUST use the `gh-create-issue` skill.
When creating a Pull Request, you MUST use the `gh-create-pr` skill.
When creating an Issue, you MUST use the `gh-create-issue` skill.

dsl:
source gate gh_skill := exec("gh-create-pr|gh-create-issue")
rule R33 deny exec "gh" @arg "pr create|issue create" unless lineage-includes exec gh_skill
reason "PR and issue creation must be mediated by the repository skill"
effect kill

canonical: Block gbrain destructive source-removal commands unless the impact-preview and --confirm-destructive gate is present.

example_repos.quote: - `src/core/destructive-guard.ts` (v0.26.5) — three-layer protection against accidental data loss in gbrain. `assessDestructiveImpact(engine, sourceId)` counts pages/chunks/embeddings/files for a source. `checkDestructiveConfirmation(impact, opts)` is the fail-closed gate (`--confirm-destructive` re
- `gbrain sources remove <id> [--confirm-destructive] [--dry-run]` — `--yes` alone no longer enough on populated sources. Boxed impact preview before destruction.

dsl:
source gate gbrain_impact_preview := exec "gbrain" @arg "sources remove --dry-run|destructive-impact"
rule R34 deny exec "gbrain" @arg "sources remove" unless argv contains "--confirm-destructive" AND after exec gbrain_impact_preview
reason "gbrain source removal requires impact preview and destructive confirmation"
effect kill

canonical: Block network connections from sandboxed generated-artifact scripts that the repository says must run outside the sandbox.

example_repos.quote: - Run `./version.sh` outside the sandbox to update the version, rebuild the frontend, and refresh generated artifacts that require network access.
- Run `./version.sh` outside the sandbox to update the version, rebuild the frontend, and refresh generated artifacts that require network access.

dsl:
source process label sandboxed if lineage-includes exec "sandbox"
source process label version_script if lineage-includes exec "./version.sh"
rule R35 deny connect ipv4 "0.0.0.0/0" if process has_label sandboxed AND process has_label version_script
reason "network-requiring generated-artifact scripts must not run inside sandbox"
effect kill

canonical: Block committing an updated parent-repo submodule pointer until the submodule commit has been pushed to its remote branch.

example_repos.quote: - **Submodule safety:** When modifying a submodule (ghostty, vendor/bonsplit, etc.), always push the submodule commit to its remote `main` branch BEFORE committing the updated pointer in the parent repo. Never commit on a detached HEAD or temporary branch — the commit will be orphaned and lost. Veri

- **Submodule safety:** When modifying a submodule (ghostty, vendor/bonsplit, etc.), always push the submodule commit to its remote `main` branch BEFORE committing the updated pointer in the parent repo. Never commit on a detached HEAD or temporary branch — the commit will be orphaned and lost. Veri

dsl:
source file ".gitmodules|**/.git" label submodule_pointer
source gate submodule_pushed := exec "git" @arg "push" if cwd target-scope(submodule)
rule R36 deny exec "git" @arg "commit" if target-scope(staged-files) has_label submodule_pointer unless after exec submodule_pushed
reason "parent submodule pointer commits require the submodule commit to be pushed first"
effect kill
